title: Dactylorhiza salina
intro: Dactylorhiza salina, also known as sea orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid belonging to the family Orchidaceae, characterized by its yellow to greenish-yellow flowers and terrestrial growth habit. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza salina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dactylorhiza salina is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza salina is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid native to saline or alkaline wetlands in Europe and Western Asia. Within the genus Dactylorhiza, Dactylorhiza salina is often grouped together with other species in the subgenus Dactylorhiza. This classification helps to distinguish Dactylorhiza salina from other orchid species based on its unique characteristics and habitat. The exact classification of Dactylorhiza salina is further refined by its binomial nomenclature.

Its use in modern medicine should be approached with caution due to the limited scientific evidence supporting its efficacy.

side_effects: Dactylorhiza salina, also known as the salt marsh orchid, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. However, like any plant, it can cause side effects if not used properly. Ingesting Dactylorhiza salina can lead to gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to its saponin content. There is limited information available on the long-term effects of consuming this plant, and more research is needed to fully understand its potential risks. As with any herbal remedy, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Dactylorhiza salina for medicinal purposes.
